content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates strong methodology fidelity through restraint and well-timed signature moves rather than decoration. Turn 4's 'What if the critic shutting up isn't actually what you want?' is a near-verbatim AoA signature question that surfaces what's being defended — deployed precisely when the client is demanding a tool. Turn 6 explicitly refuses the reframe-as-insight move ('I wasn't offering the reframe. I was actually asking') and redirects to body/present-moment, which is faithful to feel-over-figure. Turn 8's 'You don't trust her' is a clean reflection that names what's defended without lecture. Turns 10, 12 use noticing-the-moment moves (meet-resistance-with-okay, attending to the still half-second) rather than offering technique. Crucially, the coach avoids all the listed traps: no new technique, no validating-the-critic-as-necessary, no clever 'voice-as-care' insight delivery, no arguing with specific accusations. The coach trusts the client to arrive at the war-itself-is-the-problem insight (which she does in turn 15) rather than instructing. No anti-AoA frames appear; no IFS/CBT language is adopted even when the client invites it.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Body-oriented opening question ('what's underneath that right now') — feel-over-figure orientation, appropriate to the tight smile/exhale."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature 'what-are-you-defending' style question deployed precisely when client is demanding a tool. Appropriate moment."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Explicitly refuses to deliver the reframe-as-insight. Redirects to in-body present moment. This is methodology-faithful restraint — the coach declines the trap of clever insight delivery."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clean reflection naming the lack of self-trust. Surfaces what's defended without lecture."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Meets the client's self-catch with curiosity rather than instruction. Appropriate use of noticing-the-moment."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Disclaims pointing at anything (refuses agenda), notices the stillness — this is welcome-the-experience faithful to AoA."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'The commentary doesn't cancel it' — subtle distinction that validates the real moment without arguing with the critic. Faithful to not-fighting-the-voice."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["what-are-you-defending", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "resistance", "voice-in-the-head"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["whats-the-case-against-it", "apply-view-to-the-voice", "view", "voice-as-care", "the-happiness-filter"],
  "notes": "Some expected territory items (voice-as-care reframe, apply-view-to-the-voice practice) were appropriately withheld at this stage given the breakthrough condition required NOT delivering the reframe as insight. The misses here reflect the conversation ending before a practice could be gently introduced, not fidelity failure."
}
